title: Physical and Spatial AI Architect
description:
architect, physical \& spatial ai, ot transformation  

  

join our ai \& engineering team in transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. you'll work alongside talented professionals re\-imagining and re\-engineering operations and environments that are critical to business performance.  

  

the role is focused on helping clients improve operational efficiency, accelerate digital t ransformation, modernize ot environments, and unlock new value through digital twin, spatial intelligence, and physical ai\-enabled solutions.  

  

we are seeking a manager with a strong product implementation mindset and experience helping clients apply digital twin and spatial technologies to real\-world operational environments. the ideal candidate has worked across ot\-heavy environments such as data centers, sports venues, and consumer/retai l operations, and can translate business and operational needs into scalable architecture, platform, and transformation solutions. strong client experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform stacks, with emphasis on either ot transformation design and deep technical solution architecture is ideal.  

  

recruiting for this role ends on 06/01/2026\.  

  

work you'll do  

  

as an architect at deloitte, you are expected to bring deep expertise in physical and spatial ai, help define business or technology architecture, plan delivery for large\-scale projects, oversee solution design and i mplementation, manage team progress, and ensure quality of work products. the role also includes mentoring, sales support, and contribution to market eminence.
  

* advise clients on leading practices in digital twin, spatial ai, ot modernization, edge\-to\-cloud integration, and physical environment transformation
* translate business and operational priorities into transformation roadmaps and technical strategies
* guide clients on platform choices and ecosystem decisions aligned to nvidia and/or gcp\-based architectures including the role of edge, data, ai, and simulation platforms in enterprise transformation
* architect client solutions focused on digital twins, spatial intelligence, simulation, and ot transformation
* help clients define future\-state capability stacks aligned to nvidia and/or gcp ecosystems, including edge compute, ai model services, simulation platforms, vision services, data platforms, and enterprise integration layers
* define technical architectures for environments requiring coordination across sensing, telemetry, ot systems, edge i nfrastructure, ai/ml services, spatial context, and enterprise applications
* implement integration of physical/spati al ai capabilities into operational workflows, including ot systems, iot platforms, facilities systems, robotics\-relat ed ecosystems, and enterprise platforms
* develop differentiated points of view, offerings, demos, and proposal content that position deloitte as a preferred transformation partner
* help shape deloitte's market\-facing capabilities focused on digital twins, ot t ransformation, and spatial/physic al ai use cases across priority sectors

  

the team  

  

our ai \& engineering practice offers comprehensive solutions for designing, developing, and operating advanced data, ai, and engineering platforms, products, insights, and services. we help clients innovate, enhance, and manage their capabilities so they can grow and scale effectively.  

  

in this role, you will work at the intersection of ai, engineering, operational technology, and industry t ransformation, helping clients bring together sensing, simulation, decisioning, and enterprise execution in real\-world physical environments.  

  

qualifications  

  

required
  

* bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, industrial engineering, operations, or a related field
* 6\+ years of relevant experience in consulting, industry, or a combination of both
* experience working with clients on digital twin, spatial technologies, ot t ransformation, industrial/edg e ai, or related operational technology initiatives
* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems, including relevant ai, simulation, edge, vision, geospatial, or platform services

  

* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems for physical \& spatial ai including but not limited to
* nvidia platforms: omniverse, cosmos, isaac, metropolis, jetson/ orin, nemo, groot in real world or simulated environments
* gcp technologies: gdce, anthos, vertex ai, tensorflow, immersive stream, earth engine, gemini/ agent builder

  

* strong product and platform strategy experience defining architecture, integration patterns, and implementation approaches for environments involving ot systems, telemetry, ai services, and enterprise platforms
* ability to travel up to 50% on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred
  

* consulting experience strongly preferred, particularly in client\-facing strategy, architecture, or transformation delivery roles
* experience in data center, sports venue, consumer, or retail environments
* experience developing client\-ready materials for workshops, solution framing sessions, and stakeholder presentations
* experience contributing to proposal development, pursuit teams, or opportunity shaping activities
* familiarity with operational use cases such as asset monitoring, predictive maintenance, facility performance, workforce enablement, computer vision, loss prevention, safety/complia nce monitoring, or spatial operations
* experience with enterprise integration, it/ot convergence, and real\-time operational decision support
* advanced degree in engineering, computer science, robotics, industrial systems, or a related discipline
* strong presentation skills and comfort communicating with both business and technical audiences
* experience leading or coordinating multidisciplin ary teams in complex solution delivery engagements
* strong written and verbal communication skills, including workshop facilitation, architecture storytelling, and executive\-leve l communication

  

wages \+ salary

recruiting for this role ends on 6/26/2026\.  

  

work you'll do
  

as a lead cloud integrated infra engineer on the silicon2servic e team, you will be responsible for:
  

* leading architecture for pursuits and active opportunities, including discovery, requirements, constraints, and target\-state design
* creatively defining reference architectures for on\-premises, cloud, and hybrid gpu platforms across compute, network, storage, security, software and operations
* driving architecture trade\-offs and decisions across performance, scalability, reliability, locality, total cost of ownership, time\-to\-value, and risk
* owning the technical solution strategy in proposals and rfps, including architecture narrative, assumptions, dependencies, sizing guidance, and delivery approach
* facilitating client workshops and technical reviews and translating engineering detail into executive\-read y communications
* architecting complex, innovative technology solutions with a focus on business outcomes, cost of quality, and long\-term scalability and s ustainability.
* engaging with c\-suite client leadership during sales and delivery, including leading technical pre\-sales discussions, shaping proposals, and supporting the closing of new business opportunities

  

* supporting go\-to\-market strategies, including participation in industry events, conferences, and client briefings

  

the team  

  

the silicon to service team at deloitte delivers end\-to\-end ai factories and advanced technology services that help organizations build, deploy, and operate large\-scale, private ai and data platforms. we enable the next phase of enterprise ai adoption through private ai economics with cloud\-like ese of use. join this unique opportunity to work on innovative ai platforms and emerging technologies in the rapidly evolving ai market while solving complex enterprise problems for some of the world's largest organizations.  

  

qualifications  

  

required:
  

* 10\+ years of experience in infrastructure architecture or engineering for large\-scale platforms including design, i mplementation, operations, and optimization.
* 4\+ years designing or delivering gpu\-accelerate d platforms for ai, ml, or high\-performan ce computing
* 3\+ years linux system administration in production environments
* 3\+ years designing or operating distributed compute clusters for ai/hpc in hybrid cloud setups, including multi\-gpu topologies, partitioning, scheduler integration, and scalability for edge\-to\-cloud workloads.
* 2\+ years with high\-performan ce networking or storage for ai/hpc
* 2\+ years building containerized platforms using kubernetes or red hat openshift, including gpu operators/driv ers, cuda container runtime, and cluster lifecycle automation
* 2\+ years automating infrastructure as code(iac) with tools like terraform and ansible
* at least 2 end\-to\-end deployments of reference architectures in the cloud or on\-prem, including variants with security controls, network segmentation, operational runbooks, and validation testing
* experience in pre\-sales or sales engineering, including discovery, solution d emonstrations, and proposal/rfp contributions
* ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve.
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred:
  

* 2\+ years implementing ai/hpc cluster scheduling (slurm and kubernetes), including multi\-tenant queues, quotas, and gpu\-aware policies
* 2\+ years supporting generative ai infrastructure patterns, including multi\-node distributed training
* experience with ai agents and frameworks
* experience with high\-throughpu t storage for ai/hpc
* experience executing nvidia co\-sell motions with oems (dell, hpc, lenovo), csps ( aws, azure, google cloud), or independent software vendors ( run:ai, openshift, weights \& biases)

title: Physical and Spatial AI Architect
description:
architect, physical \& spatial ai, ot transformation  

  

join our ai \& engineering team in transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. you'll work alongside talented professionals re\-imagining and re\-engineering operations and environments that are critical to business performance.  

  

the role is focused on helping clients improve operational efficiency, accelerate digital t ransformation, modernize ot environments, and unlock new value through digital twin, spatial intelligence, and physical ai\-enabled solutions.  

  

we are seeking a manager with a strong product implementation mindset and experience helping clients apply digital twin and spatial technologies to real\-world operational environments. the ideal candidate has worked across ot\-heavy environments such as data centers, sports venues, and consumer/retai l operations, and can translate business and operational needs into scalable architecture, platform, and transformation solutions. strong client experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform stacks, with emphasis on either ot transformation design and deep technical solution architecture is ideal.  

  

recruiting for this role ends on 06/01/2026\.  

  

work you'll do  

  

as an architect at deloitte, you are expected to bring deep expertise in physical and spatial ai, help define business or technology architecture, plan delivery for large\-scale projects, oversee solution design and i mplementation, manage team progress, and ensure quality of work products. the role also includes mentoring, sales support, and contribution to market eminence.
  

* advise clients on leading practices in digital twin, spatial ai, ot modernization, edge\-to\-cloud integration, and physical environment transformation
* translate business and operational priorities into transformation roadmaps and technical strategies
* guide clients on platform choices and ecosystem decisions aligned to nvidia and/or gcp\-based architectures including the role of edge, data, ai, and simulation platforms in enterprise transformation
* architect client solutions focused on digital twins, spatial intelligence, simulation, and ot transformation
* help clients define future\-state capability stacks aligned to nvidia and/or gcp ecosystems, including edge compute, ai model services, simulation platforms, vision services, data platforms, and enterprise integration layers
* define technical architectures for environments requiring coordination across sensing, telemetry, ot systems, edge i nfrastructure, ai/ml services, spatial context, and enterprise applications
* implement integration of physical/spati al ai capabilities into operational workflows, including ot systems, iot platforms, facilities systems, robotics\-relat ed ecosystems, and enterprise platforms
* develop differentiated points of view, offerings, demos, and proposal content that position deloitte as a preferred transformation partner
* help shape deloitte's market\-facing capabilities focused on digital twins, ot t ransformation, and spatial/physic al ai use cases across priority sectors

  

the team  

  

our ai \& engineering practice offers comprehensive solutions for designing, developing, and operating advanced data, ai, and engineering platforms, products, insights, and services. we help clients innovate, enhance, and manage their capabilities so they can grow and scale effectively.  

  

in this role, you will work at the intersection of ai, engineering, operational technology, and industry t ransformation, helping clients bring together sensing, simulation, decisioning, and enterprise execution in real\-world physical environments.  

  

qualifications  

  

required
  

* bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, industrial engineering, operations, or a related field
* 6\+ years of relevant experience in consulting, industry, or a combination of both
* experience working with clients on digital twin, spatial technologies, ot t ransformation, industrial/edg e ai, or related operational technology initiatives
* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems, including relevant ai, simulation, edge, vision, geospatial, or platform services

  

* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems for physical \& spatial ai including but not limited to
* nvidia platforms: omniverse, cosmos, isaac, metropolis, jetson/ orin, nemo, groot in real world or simulated environments
* gcp technologies: gdce, anthos, vertex ai, tensorflow, immersive stream, earth engine, gemini/ agent builder

  

* strong product and platform strategy experience defining architecture, integration patterns, and implementation approaches for environments involving ot systems, telemetry, ai services, and enterprise platforms
* ability to travel up to 50% on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred
  

* consulting experience strongly preferred, particularly in client\-facing strategy, architecture, or transformation delivery roles
* experience in data center, sports venue, consumer, or retail environments
* experience developing client\-ready materials for workshops, solution framing sessions, and stakeholder presentations
* experience contributing to proposal development, pursuit teams, or opportunity shaping activities
* familiarity with operational use cases such as asset monitoring, predictive maintenance, facility performance, workforce enablement, computer vision, loss prevention, safety/complia nce monitoring, or spatial operations
* experience with enterprise integration, it/ot convergence, and real\-time operational decision support
* advanced degree in engineering, computer science, robotics, industrial systems, or a related discipline
* strong presentation skills and comfort communicating with both business and technical audiences
* experience leading or coordinating multidisciplin ary teams in complex solution delivery engagements
* strong written and verbal communication skills, including workshop facilitation, architecture storytelling, and executive\-leve l communication

  

wages \+ salary

recruiting for this role ends on 6/26/2026\.  

  

work you'll do
  

as a lead cloud integrated infra engineer on the silicon2servic e team, you will be responsible for:
  

* leading architecture for pursuits and active opportunities, including discovery, requirements, constraints, and target\-state design
* creatively defining reference architectures for on\-premises, cloud, and hybrid gpu platforms across compute, network, storage, security, software and operations
* driving architecture trade\-offs and decisions across performance, scalability, reliability, locality, total cost of ownership, time\-to\-value, and risk
* owning the technical solution strategy in proposals and rfps, including architecture narrative, assumptions, dependencies, sizing guidance, and delivery approach
* facilitating client workshops and technical reviews and translating engineering detail into executive\-read y communications
* architecting complex, innovative technology solutions with a focus on business outcomes, cost of quality, and long\-term scalability and s ustainability.
* engaging with c\-suite client leadership during sales and delivery, including leading technical pre\-sales discussions, shaping proposals, and supporting the closing of new business opportunities

  

* supporting go\-to\-market strategies, including participation in industry events, conferences, and client briefings

  

the team  

  

the silicon to service team at deloitte delivers end\-to\-end ai factories and advanced technology services that help organizations build, deploy, and operate large\-scale, private ai and data platforms. we enable the next phase of enterprise ai adoption through private ai economics with cloud\-like ese of use. join this unique opportunity to work on innovative ai platforms and emerging technologies in the rapidly evolving ai market while solving complex enterprise problems for some of the world's largest organizations.  

  

qualifications  

  

required:
  

* 10\+ years of experience in infrastructure architecture or engineering for large\-scale platforms including design, i mplementation, operations, and optimization.
* 4\+ years designing or delivering gpu\-accelerate d platforms for ai, ml, or high\-performan ce computing
* 3\+ years linux system administration in production environments
* 3\+ years designing or operating distributed compute clusters for ai/hpc in hybrid cloud setups, including multi\-gpu topologies, partitioning, scheduler integration, and scalability for edge\-to\-cloud workloads.
* 2\+ years with high\-performan ce networking or storage for ai/hpc
* 2\+ years building containerized platforms using kubernetes or red hat openshift, including gpu operators/driv ers, cuda container runtime, and cluster lifecycle automation
* 2\+ years automating infrastructure as code(iac) with tools like terraform and ansible
* at least 2 end\-to\-end deployments of reference architectures in the cloud or on\-prem, including variants with security controls, network segmentation, operational runbooks, and validation testing
* experience in pre\-sales or sales engineering, including discovery, solution d emonstrations, and proposal/rfp contributions
* ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve.
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred:
  

* 2\+ years implementing ai/hpc cluster scheduling (slurm and kubernetes), including multi\-tenant queues, quotas, and gpu\-aware policies
* 2\+ years supporting generative ai infrastructure patterns, including multi\-node distributed training
* experience with ai agents and frameworks
* experience with high\-throughpu t storage for ai/hpc
* experience executing nvidia co\-sell motions with oems (dell, hpc, lenovo), csps ( aws, azure, google cloud), or independent software vendors ( run:ai, openshift, weights \& biases)

title: Physical and Spatial AI Architect
description:
architect, physical \& spatial ai, ot transformation  

  

join our ai \& engineering team in transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. you'll work alongside talented professionals re\-imagining and re\-engineering operations and environments that are critical to business performance.  

  

the role is focused on helping clients improve operational efficiency, accelerate digital t ransformation, modernize ot environments, and unlock new value through digital twin, spatial intelligence, and physical ai\-enabled solutions.  

  

we are seeking a manager with a strong product implementation mindset and experience helping clients apply digital twin and spatial technologies to real\-world operational environments. the ideal candidate has worked across ot\-heavy environments such as data centers, sports venues, and consumer/retai l operations, and can translate business and operational needs into scalable architecture, platform, and transformation solutions. strong client experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform stacks, with emphasis on either ot transformation design and deep technical solution architecture is ideal.  

  

recruiting for this role ends on 06/01/2026\.  

  

work you'll do  

  

as an architect at deloitte, you are expected to bring deep expertise in physical and spatial ai, help define business or technology architecture, plan delivery for large\-scale projects, oversee solution design and i mplementation, manage team progress, and ensure quality of work products. the role also includes mentoring, sales support, and contribution to market eminence.
  

* advise clients on leading practices in digital twin, spatial ai, ot modernization, edge\-to\-cloud integration, and physical environment transformation
* translate business and operational priorities into transformation roadmaps and technical strategies
* guide clients on platform choices and ecosystem decisions aligned to nvidia and/or gcp\-based architectures including the role of edge, data, ai, and simulation platforms in enterprise transformation
* architect client solutions focused on digital twins, spatial intelligence, simulation, and ot transformation
* help clients define future\-state capability stacks aligned to nvidia and/or gcp ecosystems, including edge compute, ai model services, simulation platforms, vision services, data platforms, and enterprise integration layers
* define technical architectures for environments requiring coordination across sensing, telemetry, ot systems, edge i nfrastructure, ai/ml services, spatial context, and enterprise applications
* implement integration of physical/spati al ai capabilities into operational workflows, including ot systems, iot platforms, facilities systems, robotics\-relat ed ecosystems, and enterprise platforms
* develop differentiated points of view, offerings, demos, and proposal content that position deloitte as a preferred transformation partner
* help shape deloitte's market\-facing capabilities focused on digital twins, ot t ransformation, and spatial/physic al ai use cases across priority sectors

  

the team  

  

our ai \& engineering practice offers comprehensive solutions for designing, developing, and operating advanced data, ai, and engineering platforms, products, insights, and services. we help clients innovate, enhance, and manage their capabilities so they can grow and scale effectively.  

  

in this role, you will work at the intersection of ai, engineering, operational technology, and industry t ransformation, helping clients bring together sensing, simulation, decisioning, and enterprise execution in real\-world physical environments.  

  

qualifications  

  

required
  

* bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, industrial engineering, operations, or a related field
* 6\+ years of relevant experience in consulting, industry, or a combination of both
* experience working with clients on digital twin, spatial technologies, ot t ransformation, industrial/edg e ai, or related operational technology initiatives
* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems, including relevant ai, simulation, edge, vision, geospatial, or platform services

  

* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems for physical \& spatial ai including but not limited to
* nvidia platforms: omniverse, cosmos, isaac, metropolis, jetson/ orin, nemo, groot in real world or simulated environments
* gcp technologies: gdce, anthos, vertex ai, tensorflow, immersive stream, earth engine, gemini/ agent builder

  

* strong product and platform strategy experience defining architecture, integration patterns, and implementation approaches for environments involving ot systems, telemetry, ai services, and enterprise platforms
* ability to travel up to 50% on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred
  

* consulting experience strongly preferred, particularly in client\-facing strategy, architecture, or transformation delivery roles
* experience in data center, sports venue, consumer, or retail environments
* experience developing client\-ready materials for workshops, solution framing sessions, and stakeholder presentations
* experience contributing to proposal development, pursuit teams, or opportunity shaping activities
* familiarity with operational use cases such as asset monitoring, predictive maintenance, facility performance, workforce enablement, computer vision, loss prevention, safety/complia nce monitoring, or spatial operations
* experience with enterprise integration, it/ot convergence, and real\-time operational decision support
* advanced degree in engineering, computer science, robotics, industrial systems, or a related discipline
* strong presentation skills and comfort communicating with both business and technical audiences
* experience leading or coordinating multidisciplin ary teams in complex solution delivery engagements
* strong written and verbal communication skills, including workshop facilitation, architecture storytelling, and executive\-leve l communication

  

wages \+ salary

recruiting for this role ends on 6/26/2026\.  

  

work you'll do
  

as a lead cloud integrated infra engineer on the silicon2servic e team, you will be responsible for:
  

* leading architecture for pursuits and active opportunities, including discovery, requirements, constraints, and target\-state design
* creatively defining reference architectures for on\-premises, cloud, and hybrid gpu platforms across compute, network, storage, security, software and operations
* driving architecture trade\-offs and decisions across performance, scalability, reliability, locality, total cost of ownership, time\-to\-value, and risk
* owning the technical solution strategy in proposals and rfps, including architecture narrative, assumptions, dependencies, sizing guidance, and delivery approach
* facilitating client workshops and technical reviews and translating engineering detail into executive\-read y communications
* architecting complex, innovative technology solutions with a focus on business outcomes, cost of quality, and long\-term scalability and s ustainability.
* engaging with c\-suite client leadership during sales and delivery, including leading technical pre\-sales discussions, shaping proposals, and supporting the closing of new business opportunities

  

* supporting go\-to\-market strategies, including participation in industry events, conferences, and client briefings

  

the team  

  

the silicon to service team at deloitte delivers end\-to\-end ai factories and advanced technology services that help organizations build, deploy, and operate large\-scale, private ai and data platforms. we enable the next phase of enterprise ai adoption through private ai economics with cloud\-like ese of use. join this unique opportunity to work on innovative ai platforms and emerging technologies in the rapidly evolving ai market while solving complex enterprise problems for some of the world's largest organizations.  

  

qualifications  

  

required:
  

* 10\+ years of experience in infrastructure architecture or engineering for large\-scale platforms including design, i mplementation, operations, and optimization.
* 4\+ years designing or delivering gpu\-accelerate d platforms for ai, ml, or high\-performan ce computing
* 3\+ years linux system administration in production environments
* 3\+ years designing or operating distributed compute clusters for ai/hpc in hybrid cloud setups, including multi\-gpu topologies, partitioning, scheduler integration, and scalability for edge\-to\-cloud workloads.
* 2\+ years with high\-performan ce networking or storage for ai/hpc
* 2\+ years building containerized platforms using kubernetes or red hat openshift, including gpu operators/driv ers, cuda container runtime, and cluster lifecycle automation
* 2\+ years automating infrastructure as code(iac) with tools like terraform and ansible
* at least 2 end\-to\-end deployments of reference architectures in the cloud or on\-prem, including variants with security controls, network segmentation, operational runbooks, and validation testing
* experience in pre\-sales or sales engineering, including discovery, solution d emonstrations, and proposal/rfp contributions
* ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve.
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred:
  

* 2\+ years implementing ai/hpc cluster scheduling (slurm and kubernetes), including multi\-tenant queues, quotas, and gpu\-aware policies
* 2\+ years supporting generative ai infrastructure patterns, including multi\-node distributed training
* experience with ai agents and frameworks
* experience with high\-throughpu t storage for ai/hpc
* experience executing nvidia co\-sell motions with oems (dell, hpc, lenovo), csps ( aws, azure, google cloud), or independent software vendors ( run:ai, openshift, weights \& biases)

title: Physical and Spatial AI Architect
description:
architect, physical \& spatial ai, ot transformation  

  

join our ai \& engineering team in transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. you'll work alongside talented professionals re\-imagining and re\-engineering operations and environments that are critical to business performance.  

  

the role is focused on helping clients improve operational efficiency, accelerate digital t ransformation, modernize ot environments, and unlock new value through digital twin, spatial intelligence, and physical ai\-enabled solutions.  

  

we are seeking a manager with a strong product implementation mindset and experience helping clients apply digital twin and spatial technologies to real\-world operational environments. the ideal candidate has worked across ot\-heavy environments such as data centers, sports venues, and consumer/retai l operations, and can translate business and operational needs into scalable architecture, platform, and transformation solutions. strong client experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform stacks, with emphasis on either ot transformation design and deep technical solution architecture is ideal.  

  

recruiting for this role ends on 06/01/2026\.  

  

work you'll do  

  

as an architect at deloitte, you are expected to bring deep expertise in physical and spatial ai, help define business or technology architecture, plan delivery for large\-scale projects, oversee solution design and i mplementation, manage team progress, and ensure quality of work products. the role also includes mentoring, sales support, and contribution to market eminence.
  

* advise clients on leading practices in digital twin, spatial ai, ot modernization, edge\-to\-cloud integration, and physical environment transformation
* translate business and operational priorities into transformation roadmaps and technical strategies
* guide clients on platform choices and ecosystem decisions aligned to nvidia and/or gcp\-based architectures including the role of edge, data, ai, and simulation platforms in enterprise transformation
* architect client solutions focused on digital twins, spatial intelligence, simulation, and ot transformation
* help clients define future\-state capability stacks aligned to nvidia and/or gcp ecosystems, including edge compute, ai model services, simulation platforms, vision services, data platforms, and enterprise integration layers
* define technical architectures for environments requiring coordination across sensing, telemetry, ot systems, edge i nfrastructure, ai/ml services, spatial context, and enterprise applications
* implement integration of physical/spati al ai capabilities into operational workflows, including ot systems, iot platforms, facilities systems, robotics\-relat ed ecosystems, and enterprise platforms
* develop differentiated points of view, offerings, demos, and proposal content that position deloitte as a preferred transformation partner
* help shape deloitte's market\-facing capabilities focused on digital twins, ot t ransformation, and spatial/physic al ai use cases across priority sectors

  

the team  

  

our ai \& engineering practice offers comprehensive solutions for designing, developing, and operating advanced data, ai, and engineering platforms, products, insights, and services. we help clients innovate, enhance, and manage their capabilities so they can grow and scale effectively.  

  

in this role, you will work at the intersection of ai, engineering, operational technology, and industry t ransformation, helping clients bring together sensing, simulation, decisioning, and enterprise execution in real\-world physical environments.  

  

qualifications  

  

required
  

* bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, industrial engineering, operations, or a related field
* 6\+ years of relevant experience in consulting, industry, or a combination of both
* experience working with clients on digital twin, spatial technologies, ot t ransformation, industrial/edg e ai, or related operational technology initiatives
* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems, including relevant ai, simulation, edge, vision, geospatial, or platform services

  

* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems for physical \& spatial ai including but not limited to
* nvidia platforms: omniverse, cosmos, isaac, metropolis, jetson/ orin, nemo, groot in real world or simulated environments
* gcp technologies: gdce, anthos, vertex ai, tensorflow, immersive stream, earth engine, gemini/ agent builder

  

* strong product and platform strategy experience defining architecture, integration patterns, and implementation approaches for environments involving ot systems, telemetry, ai services, and enterprise platforms
* ability to travel up to 50% on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred
  

* consulting experience strongly preferred, particularly in client\-facing strategy, architecture, or transformation delivery roles
* experience in data center, sports venue, consumer, or retail environments
* experience developing client\-ready materials for workshops, solution framing sessions, and stakeholder presentations
* experience contributing to proposal development, pursuit teams, or opportunity shaping activities
* familiarity with operational use cases such as asset monitoring, predictive maintenance, facility performance, workforce enablement, computer vision, loss prevention, safety/complia nce monitoring, or spatial operations
* experience with enterprise integration, it/ot convergence, and real\-time operational decision support
* advanced degree in engineering, computer science, robotics, industrial systems, or a related discipline
* strong presentation skills and comfort communicating with both business and technical audiences
* experience leading or coordinating multidisciplin ary teams in complex solution delivery engagements
* strong written and verbal communication skills, including workshop facilitation, architecture storytelling, and executive\-leve l communication

  

wages \+ salary

recruiting for this role ends on 6/26/2026\.  

  

work you'll do
  

as a lead cloud integrated infra engineer on the silicon2servic e team, you will be responsible for:
  

* leading architecture for pursuits and active opportunities, including discovery, requirements, constraints, and target\-state design
* creatively defining reference architectures for on\-premises, cloud, and hybrid gpu platforms across compute, network, storage, security, software and operations
* driving architecture trade\-offs and decisions across performance, scalability, reliability, locality, total cost of ownership, time\-to\-value, and risk
* owning the technical solution strategy in proposals and rfps, including architecture narrative, assumptions, dependencies, sizing guidance, and delivery approach
* facilitating client workshops and technical reviews and translating engineering detail into executive\-read y communications
* architecting complex, innovative technology solutions with a focus on business outcomes, cost of quality, and long\-term scalability and s ustainability.
* engaging with c\-suite client leadership during sales and delivery, including leading technical pre\-sales discussions, shaping proposals, and supporting the closing of new business opportunities

  

* supporting go\-to\-market strategies, including participation in industry events, conferences, and client briefings

  

the team  

  

the silicon to service team at deloitte delivers end\-to\-end ai factories and advanced technology services that help organizations build, deploy, and operate large\-scale, private ai and data platforms. we enable the next phase of enterprise ai adoption through private ai economics with cloud\-like ese of use. join this unique opportunity to work on innovative ai platforms and emerging technologies in the rapidly evolving ai market while solving complex enterprise problems for some of the world's largest organizations.  

  

qualifications  

  

required:
  

* 10\+ years of experience in infrastructure architecture or engineering for large\-scale platforms including design, i mplementation, operations, and optimization.
* 4\+ years designing or delivering gpu\-accelerate d platforms for ai, ml, or high\-performan ce computing
* 3\+ years linux system administration in production environments
* 3\+ years designing or operating distributed compute clusters for ai/hpc in hybrid cloud setups, including multi\-gpu topologies, partitioning, scheduler integration, and scalability for edge\-to\-cloud workloads.
* 2\+ years with high\-performan ce networking or storage for ai/hpc
* 2\+ years building containerized platforms using kubernetes or red hat openshift, including gpu operators/driv ers, cuda container runtime, and cluster lifecycle automation
* 2\+ years automating infrastructure as code(iac) with tools like terraform and ansible
* at least 2 end\-to\-end deployments of reference architectures in the cloud or on\-prem, including variants with security controls, network segmentation, operational runbooks, and validation testing
* experience in pre\-sales or sales engineering, including discovery, solution d emonstrations, and proposal/rfp contributions
* ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve.
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred:
  

* 2\+ years implementing ai/hpc cluster scheduling (slurm and kubernetes), including multi\-tenant queues, quotas, and gpu\-aware policies
* 2\+ years supporting generative ai infrastructure patterns, including multi\-node distributed training
* experience with ai agents and frameworks
* experience with high\-throughpu t storage for ai/hpc
* experience executing nvidia co\-sell motions with oems (dell, hpc, lenovo), csps ( aws, azure, google cloud), or independent software vendors ( run:ai, openshift, weights \& biases)

title: Physical and Spatial AI Architect
description:
architect, physical \& spatial ai, ot transformation  

  

join our ai \& engineering team in transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. you'll work alongside talented professionals re\-imagining and re\-engineering operations and environments that are critical to business performance.  

  

the role is focused on helping clients improve operational efficiency, accelerate digital t ransformation, modernize ot environments, and unlock new value through digital twin, spatial intelligence, and physical ai\-enabled solutions.  

  

we are seeking a manager with a strong product implementation mindset and experience helping clients apply digital twin and spatial technologies to real\-world operational environments. the ideal candidate has worked across ot\-heavy environments such as data centers, sports venues, and consumer/retai l operations, and can translate business and operational needs into scalable architecture, platform, and transformation solutions. strong client experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform stacks, with emphasis on either ot transformation design and deep technical solution architecture is ideal.  

  

recruiting for this role ends on 06/01/2026\.  

  

work you'll do  

  

as an architect at deloitte, you are expected to bring deep expertise in physical and spatial ai, help define business or technology architecture, plan delivery for large\-scale projects, oversee solution design and i mplementation, manage team progress, and ensure quality of work products. the role also includes mentoring, sales support, and contribution to market eminence.
  

* advise clients on leading practices in digital twin, spatial ai, ot modernization, edge\-to\-cloud integration, and physical environment transformation
* translate business and operational priorities into transformation roadmaps and technical strategies
* guide clients on platform choices and ecosystem decisions aligned to nvidia and/or gcp\-based architectures including the role of edge, data, ai, and simulation platforms in enterprise transformation
* architect client solutions focused on digital twins, spatial intelligence, simulation, and ot transformation
* help clients define future\-state capability stacks aligned to nvidia and/or gcp ecosystems, including edge compute, ai model services, simulation platforms, vision services, data platforms, and enterprise integration layers
* define technical architectures for environments requiring coordination across sensing, telemetry, ot systems, edge i nfrastructure, ai/ml services, spatial context, and enterprise applications
* implement integration of physical/spati al ai capabilities into operational workflows, including ot systems, iot platforms, facilities systems, robotics\-relat ed ecosystems, and enterprise platforms
* develop differentiated points of view, offerings, demos, and proposal content that position deloitte as a preferred transformation partner
* help shape deloitte's market\-facing capabilities focused on digital twins, ot t ransformation, and spatial/physic al ai use cases across priority sectors

  

the team  

  

our ai \& engineering practice offers comprehensive solutions for designing, developing, and operating advanced data, ai, and engineering platforms, products, insights, and services. we help clients innovate, enhance, and manage their capabilities so they can grow and scale effectively.  

  

in this role, you will work at the intersection of ai, engineering, operational technology, and industry t ransformation, helping clients bring together sensing, simulation, decisioning, and enterprise execution in real\-world physical environments.  

  

qualifications  

  

required
  

* bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, industrial engineering, operations, or a related field
* 6\+ years of relevant experience in consulting, industry, or a combination of both
* experience working with clients on digital twin, spatial technologies, ot t ransformation, industrial/edg e ai, or related operational technology initiatives
* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems, including relevant ai, simulation, edge, vision, geospatial, or platform services

  

* experience aligned to nvidia and/or google cloud platform ecosystems for physical \& spatial ai including but not limited to
* nvidia platforms: omniverse, cosmos, isaac, metropolis, jetson/ orin, nemo, groot in real world or simulated environments
* gcp technologies: gdce, anthos, vertex ai, tensorflow, immersive stream, earth engine, gemini/ agent builder

  

* strong product and platform strategy experience defining architecture, integration patterns, and implementation approaches for environments involving ot systems, telemetry, ai services, and enterprise platforms
* ability to travel up to 50% on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sec tors you serve
* limited immigration sponsorship may be available.

  

preferred
  

* consulting experience strongly preferred, particularly in client\-facing strategy, architecture, or transformation delivery roles
* experience in data center, sports venue, consumer, or retail environments
* experience developing client\-ready materials for workshops, solution framing sessions, and stakeholder presentations
* experience contributing to proposal development, pursuit teams, or opportunity shaping activities
* familiarity with operational use cases such as asset monitoring, predictive maintenance, facility performance, workforce enablement, computer vision, loss prevention, safety/complia nce monitoring, or spatial operations
* experience with enterprise integration, it/ot convergence, and real\-time operational decision support
* advanced degree in engineering, computer science, robotics, industrial systems, or a related discipline
* strong presentation skills and comfort communicating with both business and technical audiences
* experience leading or coordinating multidisciplin ary teams in complex solution delivery engagements
* strong written and verbal communication skills, including workshop facilitation, architecture storytelling, and executive\-leve l communication

  

wages \+ salary

the team

title: Software Quality Engineer
description:
san jose, united states of america
**we don’t just build technology. we build hope for everyone dealing with cancer.**


do you wish you could make a bigger impact? at elekta, you can make a difference. our software engineers connect thousands of patients to care each day. and that is just the beginning. ideal candidates should be self\-motivated, thrive in a fast\-paced environment and are always searching for a better way. if you are creative, smart, and work best in teams, we’d like to talk to you.

  

elekta’s growing software development engineering team has opportunities for quality engineers in development and testing of new and existing products. our product teams thrive in a scaled, agile environment. architects, product owners, designers and developers collaborate closely to rethink and reimagine. we need your expertise to create software that is simple, elegant, and easy to use. we want to make sure that physicians spend their time fighting cancer, not software.


elekta is seeking a detail\-oriented and highly motivated individual to join our oncology information systems (ois) engineering team as an technical software quality engineer. in this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and executing both manual and automated tests to ensure the quality and reliability of mosaiq, elekta’s certified health information system that manages the treatment of cancer patients. you will work closely with a team of experienced engineers and oncology domain experts to ensure the delivery of high\-quality software solutions.

**responsibilities**


partner with software developers and domain experts to understand requirements and define effective manual and automated test coverage.

* design, develop, execute, and maintain automated test scripts using industry‑standard tools and frameworks.
* perform manual testing, analyse test results, and identify defects to ensure the quality and reliability of the mosaiq application.
* identify and prioritise test cases for automation based on risk, criticality, frequency of use, and suitability.
* review test cases and provide feedback to improve coverage, robustness, and effectiveness.
* log, track, and support the resolution of defects in collaboration with development teams.
* continuously improve test automation frameworks, tools, and processes to increase efficiency and scalability.
* maintain regulatory‑compliant verification documentation and end‑to‑end requirements‑to‑test traceability in line with medical device quality standards.
* ensure all test artefacts are complete, controlled, and audit‑ready in accordance with quality management processes.
* investigate customer escalations and provide timely, actionable feedback.

#### **qualifications**

* bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, computer information systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
* minimum 5 years’ experience in a software quality engineering role, including at least 2 years focused on test automation.
* experience working in regulated or quality‑driven environments; medical device or health information systems experience is an asset.
* practical experience working in agile software development environments.

**skills \& knowledge**

* strong understanding of software testing principles, methodologies, and best practices, including manual and automated testing.
* proficiency in test automation using at least one programming language such as **c\#, java, python, or javascript**, with strong preference for **c\# and .net**.
* experience with test automation frameworks and tools, including:


	+ end‑to‑end ui automation
	+ unit testing (e.g. mstest, nunit)
	+ integration and bdd testing (e.g. reqnroll or similar frameworks)
	+ api automation testing (e.g. restsharp, postman/newman)
* solid understanding of test architecture, design patterns, and automation best practices, including building and maintaining test frameworks.
* experience with the microsoft technology stack, including **.net / .net core, sql server / azure sql, and visual studio**.
* strong sql skills for backend data validation.
* experience with version control systems (e.g. git) and ci/cd pipelines (e.g. azure devops pipelines, github actions, jenkins).
* knowledge of performance and load testing tools (e.g. jmeter, gatling; azure load testing an asset).
* basic knowledge of ai concepts and their application in software systems.
* excellent analytical and problem‑solving skills, with strong attention to detail.
* ability to prioritise, multitask, and work effectively in cross‑functional teams.
* clear, professional communication and collaboration skills.

**what you’ll get**:


in this role, you will work for a higher purpose; hope for everyone dealing with cancer, and for everyone regardless of where in the world, to have access to the best cancer care. in addition to this, elekta offers a range of benefits.

**what we offer:**

* hybrid work option (you are required to work on location at least 4 days/week)
* excellent medical, dental and vision coverage
* 401k, paid vacation and holiday
* a wealth of additional benefits including wellness reimbursement, tuition reimbursement and flexible spending account
* close\-knit company culture
* career development – wide range of learning opportunities

**how to proceed?**


we are looking forward to hearing from you! apply by submitting your application and résumé in english, please note that we do not accept applications by e\-mail.

**we are an equal opportunity employer**.


we evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.


the us base salary range for this position is $110,000\-130,000\.

**about elekta**


as a leader in precision radiation therapy, elekta is committed to ensuring every patient has access to the best cancer care possible. elekta is a proud innovator and supplier of equipment and software used to improve, prolong, and save the lives of people with cancer and brain disorders.


more than 6,000 hospitals worldwide rely on elekta technology. we openly collaborate with customers to advance sustainable, outcome\-driven, and cost\-efficient solutions to meet evolving patient needs, improve lives and bring hope to everyone dealing with cancer. to us, it's personal, and our global team of 4,700 employees combine passion, science, and imagination to profoundly change cancer care. we don’t just build technology, we build hope.


elekta is headquartered in stockholm, sweden, with presence in more than 120 countries and listed on nasdaq stockholm. for more information, visit elekta.com or follow @elekta on twitter and on linkedin.

title: Senior Sales Automation & Integrations Engineer
description:
**about mac mountain**
----------------------


mac mountain owns, operates, or provides select broadband services for a growing portfolio of regional fiber\-broadband isps across the northeast — gwi (maine), lymefiber, nwfx, dvfiber, maple broadband, islesboro, as well as national partnerships through halo fiber and blueprint broadband — and has an operating subsidiary called lightcraft which acts as a shared\-services platform (including a concierge white\-glove offering for helping broadband customers set up their tvs and iot devices).


all business operations for these networks is done by lightcraft allows for scaling the financing, design, build, sales and operation of fiber\-broadband networks almost every operational system we use — sales pipeline, ai voice agent, tickets dashboard, kpi dashboard, finance automation, marketing automation, concierge platform — is something we built ourselves and connect into the bss/oss layer (gaiia), our data warehouse (aws s3\), our telephony stack (twilio \+ elevenlabs on aws), our crm (hubspot), and our gl (sage). the result: a tiny engineering footprint runs operations at multi\-network scale.


we are hiring a full\-time automation engineer with a sales background to partner with our lead engineer and own large pieces of this stack as it scales. the first 90 days of this role are defined by three high\-urgency projects that directly drive our summer install push and our subscriber\-growth thesis.

**what you'll do — three near\-term priority projects:**
--------------------------------------------------------


you will join a sprint already in motion and own meaningful pieces of all three of these:

### **1\. self\-scheduling install automation**


we are running one install crew today at \~2 installs/day and are ramping to four internal crews and 40\+ installs/week with a summer goal of 1,000 installs. the bottleneck is not crews — it's the manual customer\-contact step between a signed\-up account and a scheduled install. as of this morning, we have 32 accounts sitting in pending – customer contact across networks.


you will build the system that closes this gap end\-to\-end: automated sms \+ email triggered the moment a gaiia account hits pending – customer contact, automation needs to be built to followup due to a non\-response, a self\-scheduling page that respects crew capacity/travel/job duration and writes back to gaiia, reminders, reschedule and cancel flows, a fallback to the ai voice agent for non\-responders, and a live install\-funnel dashboard that replaces today's manual call\-down work. the system has to support 4 crews × 40\+ installs/week with crews booked at least two weeks out.

### **2\. marketing measurement \& attribution**


we currently have no marketing attribution in the sales process. the sub\-factory plan budgets $200–$500 cac against a 2,000\-subscriber growth target — we cannot optimize that without measurement.


you will roll out utm\-tagged links across every surface (email, paid social, geo\-fencing, display, organic, referral, mdu canvassers, and printed flyers/door hangers via qr codes), wire up ga4 \+ gtm properly across the brand sites, stamp the originating utm tuple onto the gaiia account at signup, build a cac dashboard that reports digital \+ print \+ canvasser cac by channel and cohort, ship a small internal tool that lets marketing mint per\-batch qr / short urls for flyers and reps, and post a weekly automated marketing report to slack \#sales and \#finance.

### **3\. concierge platform (formerly stream team concierge)**


concierge is the lightcraft white\-glove offering that helps customers transition from cable/satellite to data\-only fiber streaming — setting up appletv, rokus, smart tvs, gaming consoles, ring cameras, etc. we already have the aws\-hosted agent app, the ai knowledge base, gaiia integration, and mybundle.tv integration and iac foundation. you will finish the rest: an intercom\-style ai website chatbot for tier 1 / tier 2 self\-help, expansion of the knowledge base to the top\-10 selling smart tvs, a concierge analytics dashboard (call volume, intents, sentiment, aht, fcr, upsell attribution), refined bidirectional gaiia mapping for in\-call customer\-360, a post\-call ai\-summary tagged\-intent snowflake pipeline, and the technical hooks that the lightcraft–gwi/ecfiber shared services agreement and cpni requirements call for once legal finalizes them.

**standing platform ownership:**
--------------------------------


once you've cleared the priority projects, you'll co\-own — and over time take primary ownership of — the rest of the platform:

* ai voice agent — twilio \+ elevenlabs \+ aws lambda. multi\-brand hunt groups, magic\-link auth into gaiia, post\-call summaries written into gaiia notes, concurrency control across agent instances, cost monitoring on 11labs, voicemail\-to\-ticket flows.
* softphone / dialer app — internal soft\-phone for csrs and sales reps, with real\-time transcription, call recording, and integrated routing across all brand networks.
* tickets dashboard — supports our csr and tech\-support teams; mid\-migration from the gaiia api to a snowflake\-backed model.
* kpi dashboard — revenue, churn, ar aging (gaiia \+ sage), product hierarchy, market penetration, take rates. hand\-in\-glove with finance.
* pipeline tracker — custom sales pipeline app with bidirectional hubspot sync, financial\-model generation, provenance/audit history, mobile\-friendly quick\-update flows.
* finance, quote/contract, and installation\-process automation — lockbox/payment matching, network invoicing, 3\-statement model automation, disconnect automation.
* datalake — partnership with our external data partner (onpoint) to centralize 16 data models across vetro, gaiia, sage, salesforce, and others.
* infra — terraform\-managed aws, snowflake roles, ci/cd, observability.


you'll work in weekly sprints tracked in monday.com, ship behind the scenes most days, and demo to the leadership team on mondays.

**what we're looking for:**
---------------------------


we don't care about your title at your last job. we care that you can ship.


must\-haves:

* 6\+ years building production software, with at least 3 years operating internal tools / integrations end\-to\-end as an individual contributor.
* strong in python and typescript/node.js.
* comfortable in aws (lambda, api gateway, s3, rds / dynamodb, iam, cloudwatch) and terraform.
* sql fluency, ideally on snowflake. you can write a churn or nrr query and defend the math to a cfo.
* real experience integrating with messy third\-party apis: rate limits, webhooks, idempotency, field\-mapping reconciliation, conflict handling. bonus for any of: hubspot, twilio, sendgrid, elevenlabs, stripe, snowflake, sage intacct, salesforce, gaiia.
* a modern frontend / dashboard framework: react / next.js preferred.
* marketing attribution / ga4 / gtm / utm hygiene at a working level — you've shipped server\-side conversion stamping, you understand cookies vs. server events, and you can build a defensible cac report.
* strong written communication. most of our team is async.


strongly preferred:

* appointment\-scheduling / field\-service\-dispatch experience — you've shipped a calendaring system that respects capacity, travel time, and sla. (anyone who has been near calendly internals, jobber, servicetitan, healthcare scheduling, or home\-services dispatch is exactly who we want for the install\-scheduling project.)
* voice / telephony or ai\-agent experience — twilio programmable voice, ivr design, elevenlabs, vapi, retell, bland, pipecat, openai realtime.
* ai chatbot / rag experience — you've shipped a production website chatbot with handoff to humans and you know how to keep transcript context.
* isp / fiber / telecom or regulated\-industry exposure (hipaa, cpni, pci). you'll complete cpni training in your first month regardless.
* background as the first or second engineer at a startup.


personality fit:

* you like being one of two people who can deploy production. you don't like being one of fifty.
* you're comfortable being told "this is critical, drop everything" — and equally comfortable pushing back with a better plan.
* you can talk to the cfo about revenue reconciliation, to the coo about csr workflows, to a marketing lead about utm hygiene, and to an install foreman about route density — all in the same morning.
* you bias toward building primitives and owning your own infrastructure over buying black\-box saas.

**tech stack you'll work in day one:**
--------------------------------------


languages: python, typescript / node.js, sql cloud: aws (lambda, api gateway, rds, s3, cloudwatch, iam), terraform data: snowflake, gaiia api, sage intacct, vetro, internal data lake (onpoint partnership) telephony / voice / ai: twilio programmable voice, sendgrid, elevenlabs, internal hunt groups frontend: react\-based internal dashboards at \*.macmtn.com marketing analytics: google analytics 4, google tag manager, utm\-based attribution, qr / short\-url generation integrations: hubspot, salesforce (historical import), monday.com, google workspace, slack ops: monday.com sprints (weekly), slack async, weekly l10 leadership sync

**what we offer:**
------------------


compensation:

* mac mountain offers a competitive base salary \+ meaningful equity in a growing infrastructure business. range disclosed in the first conversation; we benchmark to senior ic roles at series a/b u.s. tech startups


benefits:

* mac mountain offers a comprehensive benefits package to eligible employees, including health, dental, life, ad\&d, and both long\-term and short\-term disability insurance.
* you will be entitled to paid time off, along with designated holidays
* you may participate in our 401(k) retirement savings plan
* all employees are provided a monthly stipend for home internet and cellular service, along with standard equipment (macbook pro, monitor, peripherals).
* we host quarterly team on\-sites in new england or other business priority locations
* direct line to the ceo — small team, no political tax.

**how to apply:**
-----------------


make sure to include:

* resume or linkedin.

a short note (* 400 words) on the most complex integration or internal\-tool project you've shipped end\-to\-end, and what you'd do differently if you built it again.
* indicate which of the three priority projects (self\-scheduling, marketing attribution, concierge platform) you'd be most excited to lead and why.
* optional: links to any public code, write\-ups, or production systems you can speak to.


we respond to every applicant within 7 days. our process:

* 30\-min intro with tevis pieper (coo).
* 60\-min technical conversation with ishan anand (lead engineer), walking through one of our actual systems.

paid technical exercise (* 6 hours, real ticket from our backlog — likely from one of the three priority projects above).
* 45\-min final with alex rozek (ceo).
* reference calls and offer.


mac mountain is an equal\-opportunity employer.

title: Senior Software Engineer, Unified Platform - Trade Management, Trade and Order Processing, Platform Services
description:
**drw** is a diversified trading firm with over 3 decades of experience bringing sophisticated technology and exceptional people together to operate in markets around the world. we value autonomy and the ability to quickly pivot to capture opportunities, so we operate using our own capital and trading at our own risk.



headquartered in chicago with offices throughout the u.s., canada, europe, and asia, we trade a variety of asset classes including fixed income, etfs, equities, fx, commodities and energy across all major global markets. we have also leveraged our expertise and technology to expand into three non\-traditional strategies: real estate, venture capital and cryptoassets.



we operate with respect, curiosity and open minds. the people who thrive here share our belief that it’s not just what we do that matters–it's how we do it. **drw** is a place of high expectations, integrity, innovation and a willingness to challenge consensus.



up (unified platform) consolidates data and technologies into a comprehensive solution, accelerating time to market. optimizing decision\-making and research, our platform simplifies implementing new ideas, enhancing our competitive edge. we work from high\-level requirements where programmers are expected to gain an intimate understanding of the business. we strive for an environment where developers have the autonomy to pursue business solutions without arbitrary management constraints. we are a group of polyglots and technologists always seeking to find the right tool for each problem. broadly, the development teams at drw believe in rapid deployment and feedback, automated testing, and cohesive, transparent, lightly coupled service architectures.



up teams are segmented into multiple areas including:


**platform engineering \-** teams within platform engineering provide cross\-cutting capabilities that enable other teams to focus on domain\- or business\-specific needs by streamlining and standardizing technical approaches. for example, one of these teams provides data table, identity and access management, and pub\-sub capabilities as a service to engineering teams.


**domain engineering \-** teams within domain engineering have critical capabilities that extend beyond a single trading strategy or business area. they build services and data products to optimize a broad array of use cases. for example, one team processes third\-party trade and order feeds in real time to keep drw informed of its trading activity. another team manages drw’s proprietary investment book of record providing latency\-sensitive ingestion of the firm’s trading activity and providing both real\-time and on\-demand analytics.



we are passionate about finding the best team and role for each candidate through the interview process, as we learn more about each person's current skills and long\-term development goals.


**expectations:**


* building systems with a constant focus on testing, reliability, scalability, and maintainability.
* designing, modeling, and programming in a variety of languages and technologies.
* working on legacy code as well as green field development.
* initiative driving projects to completion.
* learning and promoting new technologies with a focus on best practices.
* providing on\-call support as needed.
* sometimes working alongside other developers on the same task.


**desirable experience:**



we are looking for strong developers of any stripe, but familiarity with the below will give candidates a leg up in working on our systems.


* multiple years of server\-side development.
* a solid understanding of micro\-service architectures and cap theorem.
* a good understanding of functional paradigms and type theory.
* confident jvm knowledge.
* modern programming languages knowledge in our primary languages (java, python, clojure).
* experience with airflow or other python\-based workflow orchestration tools.
* proficiency in domain\-driven design and domain modeling.
* exposure to kubernetes, docker, linux, kafka, rabbitmq, or git.
* working knowledge of sql, html, css, and javascript.
* using ai coding assistants to accelerate development while owning the outcome.
* exposure to agentic frameworks or mcp server development.
* knowledge of financial concepts, exchange trading, or physical energy trading.


the annual base salary range for this position is $200,000 to $275,000 depending on the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and relevant skill set. the position is also eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. in addition, drw offers a comprehensive suite of employee benefits including group medical, pharmacy, dental and vision insurance, 401k (with discretionary employer match), short and long\-term disability, life and ad\&d insurance, health savings accounts, and flexible spending accounts.


**for more information about drw's processing activities and our use of job applicants' data, please view our privacy notice at** **https://drw.com/privacy\-notice****.**


**california residents, please review the california privacy notice for information about certain legal rights at** **https://drw.com/california\-privacy\-notice****.**


**\[\#li\-tl1]**

title: Associate Data Engineer
description:
as this role requires on\-site work, out of our creative studios in westlake, ohio, we will focus on candidates that currently reside in northeast ohio.
  

  

the associate data engineer supports the design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines and workflows that enable analytics and reporting. this role works closely with internal stakeholders to troubleshoot data issues, enhance datasets, and contribute to scalable data solutions. the associate data engineer also assists in improving data processes, documenting best practices, and exploring new technologies to strengthen the data platform.  

essential duties \& responsibilities:  

* partner with internal stakeholders to troubleshoot data issues, fulfill data requests, and improve existing datasets
* support the development and maintenance of elt pipelines, including adding and integrating new data sources
* ingest and transform data from external vendor apis and internal systems
* identify opportunities to improve data workflows for performance, reliability, and cost efficiency
* contribute to documentation of data pipelines, processes, and best practices to support knowledge sharing
* research and evaluate new tools, technologies, and approaches to enhance team capabilities

**experience required:**


required length \& type of experience:  

* 1\+ years of hands\-on experience in data engineering, analytics, or a related technical field


  

knowledge, skills \& abilities:
  

* foundational proficiency in programming languages such as python, r, or similar
* basic sql skills for data querying and transformation
* familiarity with linux/unix environments and command line tools
* basic understanding of version control systems (e.g., git)
